caption: AI Agent
description: An autonomous AI agent that performs tasks under delegated authority. Distinguished from the OCSF agent object (which describes security sensors such as EDR and DLP tools) and from human principals. An AI agent has a stable logical identity across runs and a restart-sensitive instance identity. The agent owns its model — the ai_model attribute captures which model was backing the agent at the time of action.
name: ai_agent

uid.caption: AI Agent ID
uid.description: The stable logical identifier for the agent. Persists across restarts and instances.

instance_uid.caption: AI Agent Instance ID
instance_uid.description: Identifier for a specific running instance of the agent. Restart-sensitive: a new instance ID is expected after a process restart or re-spawn. Enables attribution of actions to a specific process or session of the agent.

name.description: Human-readable name for the agent. For example: Q4 Analysis Agent or Model Tester Agent.

type.caption: Agent Type
type.description: The agent framework or protocol type, normalized to the caption of the type_id value.

type_id.caption: Agent Type ID
type_id.description: The normalized identifier for the agent framework or protocol. Different agent frameworks have different identity, tool-call, and delegation semantics — recording the type enables cross-framework normalization.

0.caption: Unknown
0.description: The agent framework type is unknown.

1.caption: Native
1.description: A first-party or proprietary agent without a standardized interoperability protocol. For example, a custom orchestrator or an LLM provider's own agent SDK.

2.caption: MCP
2.description: An agent that exposes or consumes tools via the Model Context Protocol (MCP), enabling standardized tool discovery and invocation across runtimes.

3.caption: A2A
3.description: An agent that communicates using the Agent-to-Agent (A2A) protocol, enabling structured inter-agent task delegation and result exchange.

4.caption: LangChain
4.description: An agent built with the LangChain framework.

5.caption: AutoGen
5.description: An agent built with the Microsoft AutoGen framework for multi-agent conversation and orchestration.

6.caption: CrewAI
6.description: An agent built with the CrewAI framework for role-based multi-agent collaboration.

99.caption: Other
99.description: The agent framework type is not listed. Use the type attribute to describe it.

ai_model.description: The AI model backing this agent at the time of the recorded event. An agent's model may change across instances or versions; this captures the model in use for the specific logged activity or delegation.

version.caption: AI Agent Version
version.description: The version of the agent (e.g., 1.4.2). Enables detection of behavioral changes across versions and attribution of actions to a specific agent revision.

charter.caption: Agent Charter
charter.description: The role definition document for this agent — a durable file (system prompt, charter, or constitution) describing what the agent is constituted to do, the tools and scopes it may use, and the bounds within which it operates. Stable across delegations and runs; distinct from per-run goals or tasks, which belong on the delegation object.
